DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-14-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS KENDY TELLO, AND ARIEL TELLO'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO THEIR SET ONE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. NO LATER THAN MARCH 14, 2025 ON A MUTUALLY AGREEABLE DATE OR DATES AMERICAN HONDA MUST PRODUCE ALL NON-PRIVILEGED RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING WITH A COVER LETTER OUTLINING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 . JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

DISCOVERY 302, DEC-31-2024 RE: MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE. THE SUBJECT MATTER IS DISCUSSED. COUNSEL OFFERS ADVOCACY TO CONTINUE THE SUBJECT MOTION. THE COURT AGREES TO CONTINUE THE SUBJECT MATTER. NO OPPOSITION TO THE PRECEDING IS PRESENTED. THE COURT WITHDRAWS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND THE SUBJECT MATTER IS CONTINUED TO DISCOVERY ON JAN-14-2025 AT 9:00 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 302. COUNSEL IS INSTRUCTED TO DELIVER COURTESY COPIES TO DEPARTMENT 302 BY THE CLOSE OF BUSINESS TODAY, DEC-31-2024. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/CEK)

Atty. DANIEL GOPSTEIN, Esq. of Quill & Arrow, LLP (tel: 310-933-4271) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. SAMANTHA GERAGHTY, Esq. of Evans Fears Schittert McNulty Mickus (tel: 949-301-9464) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS KENDY TELLO, AND ARIEL TELLO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set One.

Matter discussed. Counsel Samantha Geraghty requests more time to produce the documents and make the PMK(s) available for deposition. Upon conclusion, the Court grants counsel Samantha Geraghty's request and changes the deadline from February 14, 2025 to March 14, 2025. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

Plaintiffs' motion to compel further responses to their set one requests for production of documents is granted. No later than March 14, 2025 on a mutually agreeable date or dates American Honda must produce all non-privileged responsive documents. American Honda must identify any documents withheld on the grounds of privilege in a privilege log. The document requests meet the standard for discovery relevance and American Honda has not shown that compliance with the requests is unduly burdensome.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter outlining compliance with CRC 3.1312 .

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/HEK)

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. DANIEL GOPSTEIN, Esq. of Quill & Arrow, LLP (tel: 310-933-4271) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. SAMANTHA GERAGHTY, Esq. of Evans Fears Schittert McNulty Mickus (tel: 949-301-9464) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS KENDY TELLO, AND ARIEL TELLO's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant's Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents.

Matter discussed. Counsel Samantha Geraghty requests more time to produce the documents and make the PMK(s) available for deposition. Upon conclusion, the Court grants counsel Samantha Geraghty's request and changes the deadline from February 14, 2025 to March 14, 2025. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

Plaintiffs' motion to compel PMK deposition and production of documents is granted. No later than March 14, 2025 on a mutually agreeable date or dates American Honda must make available for deposition one or more PMKs and produce all non-privileged responsive documents. American Honda must identify any documents withheld on the grounds of privilege in a privilege log. Plaintiffs are entitled to a PMK deposition, the matters for examination and document requests meet the standard for discovery relevance, and American Honda has not shown that compliance with the notice of deposition is unduly burdensome.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter outlining compliance with CRC 3.1312 .

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/HEK)
